name: Caramelized-Onion and Wine-Braised Brisket
recipeIngredient: Mixed baby vegetables (such as zucchini, crookneck and pattypan squashes,
4 ounces asparagus tips (or sugar snap peas)
recipeInstructions:
1. Boil the first three ingredients in a pot until reduced to 4 cups, about
25 minutes.
2. Preheat the oven to 325°F.
3. Heat oil in a large Dutch oven over high heat. Add bones and sauté until
well browned, turning occasionally, about 12 minutes. Transfer bones to a bowl.
4. Add onions, celery, and allspice to the Dutch oven. Season with salt and
cook until onions are golden, stirring often, about 25 minutes. Continue cooking
until onions are deep dark brown, scraping bottom of the Dutch oven and stirring
often, about 15 more minutes. Add garlic and thyme; sauté 5 minutes.
5. Transfer onion mixture to a large roasting pan. Add 1 cup of broth mixture
to the Dutch oven. Bring to boil, scraping up browned bits; transfer to the
roasting pan.
6. Season brisket generously with salt and pepper. Add to the Dutch oven and
brown over high heat, about 5 minutes per side. Place brisket, fat side up,
on onion mixture in the roasting pan; surround with bones. Add remaining broth
mixture and tomatoes to the Dutch oven; bring to boil, scraping up browned bits.
Pour the mixture over brisket.
7. Cover the roasting pan tightly with heavy-duty foil and place in the oven.
Bake until brisket is tender, about 3 hours and 45 minutes. Remove foil. Cool
brisket for 2 hours. Refrigerate uncovered for 3 hours. Cover brisket tightly
and keep chilled for up to 1 day or up to 3 days.
8. Scrape fat from the top of pan juices; discard fat. Transfer brisket to a cutting board,
scraping gelled juices off brisket back into the roasting pan. Bring contents
of the roasting pan to simmer. Discard bones. Pour contents of the roasting
pan into a coarse strainer set over a large bowl. Press on solids to release as
much liquid as possible. Puree solids in a processor, using on/off turns.
9. Mix enough puree into juices in the bowl to form a thick sauce. Add tomato
paste and whisk to blend. Pour sauce into a medium saucepan and simmer until
heated through. Season with salt and pepper.
10. Slice brisket thinly across grain at a slight diagonal angle. Arrange slices
in a glass baking dish. Drizzle 1/2 cup of sauce over; cover with foil.
11. Bring a large pot of salted water to boil. Add all squashes, turnips, and carrots
and cook until crisp-tender, about 6 minutes. Using a slotted spoon, transfer to
a large bowl. Add potatoes to boiling water and cook until just tender, about
12 minutes. Using a slotted spoon, transfer potatoes to the same bowl. Add onions
to boiling water and cook until almost tender, about 4 minutes. Drain and cool slightly.
Peel onions; add to the same bowl.
12. Preheat the oven to 350°F. Bake brisket until heated through, about 30 minutes.
Meanwhile, boil margarine, honey, and thyme in a heavy large skillet over high heat
until syrup is reduced to 1/3 cup, stirring often, about 5 minutes. Add cooked vegetables,
tomatoes, and asparagus; toss until heated, about 5 minutes. Season with salt and pepper.
13. Arrange brisket on a platter. Bring remaining sauce to simmer. Arrange vegetables
around brisket. Serve, passing remaining sauce separately.
8-10 Servings
